questions.question: Necessary calorie requirement for a male light worker is:
questions.explanation: Page 424: Light worker male requires 2000 calorie, female 2100 calorie.

questions.question: Necessary calorie requirement for a female eight-hour worker is:
questions.explanation: Page 424: Eight hours worker female requires 2500 calorie, male 3000 calorie.

questions.question: A male hard worker requires how many calories?
questions.explanation: Page 424: Hard worker male requires 3600 calorie, female 3000 calorie.

questions.question: Which substance is used as a preservative to preserve food items?
questions.explanation: Page 424: Sodium Benzoate is used as preservative to preserve food items.

questions.question: Milk is not considered a complete food due to lack of:
questions.explanation: Page 424: Milk is not considered as complete food due to lack of vitamin C and iron.

questions.question: Which is a genetically modified vegetable recently made available in Indian market?
questions.explanation: Page 424: Bt-Brinjal is a genetically modified vegetable recently being made available in Indian market.

questions.question: Biotechnology deals with techniques of using:

options.B: Live organisms or enzymes to produce useful products

questions.explanation: Page 424: Biotechnology deals with techniques of using live organisms or enzymes from organisms to produce useful products (e.g., curd, bread, wine).

questions.question: Genetic Engineering is the technique to alter:

options.B: Chemistry of genetic material (DNA & RNA) to change phenotype

questions.explanation: Page 424: Genetic Engineering is the technique to alter the chemistry of genetic material (DNA & RNA) and introduce into host organism, changing the phenotype.

questions.question: Bioprocess Engineering involves maintenance of:

options.B: Sterile ambience for desired microbes/eukaryotic cells

questions.explanation: Page 424: Bioprocess Engineering is maintenance of sterile ambience for growth of desired microbes or eukaryotic cells for manufacture of biotechnological products.

questions.question: Restriction enzyme is known as:
questions.explanation: Page 424: Restriction enzyme is known as molecular scissors cutting the DNA at specific location.

questions.question: Plasmid is:

options.B: Extra small circular DNA in bacteria

questions.explanation: Page 424: In addition to genomic DNA, many bacteria have extra small circular DNA known as plasmid.

questions.question: Due to presence of plasmid, bacteria show resistance to:
questions.explanation: Page 424: Due to presence of plasmid, bacteria show resistance to many antibiotics. This creates a unique character in bacteria.

questions.question: A plasmid can be used as a vector to deliver:

options.B: An alien piece of DNA into host organism

questions.explanation: Page 424: A plasmid can be used as vector to deliver an alien piece of DNA into host organism.

questions.question: DNA ligase is an enzyme that:

options.B: Joins cut ends of DNA molecule together

questions.explanation: Page 424: DNA ligase is an enzyme by which cut ends of DNA molecule join together.

questions.question: Recombinant DNA is created by:

options.B: Combining two fragments of DNA from two different sources

questions.explanation: Page 424: Recombinant DNA is a DNA which has been created by combining two fragments of DNA from two different sources.

questions.question: Cloning is the process of producing:

options.B: Large number of identical copies of any template DNA

questions.explanation: Page 424: Cloning is producing large number of identical copies of any template DNA.

questions.question: Restriction enzymes belong to a larger class of enzymes called:
questions.explanation: Page 424: Restriction enzymes belong to larger class of enzymes called nucleases.

questions.question: Exonuclease removes nucleotide from:
questions.explanation: Page 424: Exonuclease removes nucleotide from the end of DNA.

questions.question: Endonuclease cuts at:
questions.explanation: Page 424: Endonuclease cuts at specific position within the DNA.

questions.question: Organisms whose genes have been altered by manipulation are called:
questions.explanation: Page 424: Plant, bacteria, fungi and animals whose genes have been altered by manipulation are called Genetically Modified Organisms (GMO).

questions.question: Gene therapy is:

options.B: Collection of methods that allow correction of a gene defect in a child or embryo

questions.explanation: Page 424: Gene therapy is a collection of methods that allow correction of a gene defect that has been diagnosed in a child or embryo.

questions.question: The first clinical gene therapy was given in 1990 to a four-year-old girl with which deficiency?
questions.explanation: Page 424: The first clinical gene therapy was given in 1990 to a four-year-old girl with adenosine deaminase (ADA) deficiency.

questions.question: Genetically modified plants are useful in:

options.B: Increasing crop yields, reducing losses, and making crops stress tolerant

questions.explanation: Page 424: GM plants are useful in increasing crop yields, reduce losses and make the crop more tolerant to stresses.

questions.question: Papaver somniferum plant yields:
questions.explanation: Page 424: Papaver somniferum plant yields powerful analgesic.

questions.question: The major source of sugar in the world is:
questions.explanation: Page 424: Major source of sugar in the world is Saccharum Officinarum.

questions.question: Cocos nucifera is widely used in:
questions.explanation: Page 424: Cocos nucifera is a plant material widely used in preparation of culture medium.

questions.question: To yield milk, cow is given which hormone?
questions.explanation: Page 424: To yield milk, cow is given prolactin.

questions.question: The best way to determine paternity is:
questions.explanation: Page 424: The best way to determine paternity is DNA finger printing.

questions.question: Chloromycetin is obtained from:
questions.explanation: Page 424: Chloromycetin is obtained from streptomycetin venezuelae.

questions.question: In Recombinant Vector Vaccines, which are used as vectors?
questions.explanation: Page 424: Genetic engineering is applied in development of Recombinant Vector Vaccines in which bacteria and virus are used as vectors.

questions.question: Bollgard I and Bollgard II technology are used in:
questions.explanation: Page 424: Bollgard I and Bollgard II technology are used in developing genetically modified crop plants.

questions.question: The term 'ACE-2' is talked about in the context of:
questions.explanation: Page 424: The term ACE-2 is talked about in the context of spread of viral diseases.

questions.question: A diagnostic test, Real Time PCR for COVID-19 detects:
questions.explanation: Page 424: Real Time PCR for COVID-19 detects RNA of virus.

questions.question: Rapid Antibodies Test does not require sample from nose and throat and was approved by:
questions.explanation: Page 424: Rapid Antibodies Test does not require sample from nose and throat in COVID-19 test, approved by Indian Council of Medical Research.

questions.question: Vector vaccines work by:

options.B: Entering cells and enabling them to create spike protein

questions.explanation: Page 424: Vector vaccines work to provide immunity by entering directly into the cells and enabling them to create spike protein.

questions.question: Sinovac given for COVID-19 is a:
questions.explanation: Page 424: Sinovac given for Covid-19 is a whole virus vaccine.

questions.question: DNA Barcoding is a tool to:

options.B: Distinguish among species that look alike

questions.explanation: Page 424: DNA Barcoding can be a tool to distinguish among species that look alike.

questions.question: Covishield vaccine was produced by Serum Institute of India using which platform?
questions.explanation: Page 424: The Serum Institute of India produced Covishield using mRNA platform.

questions.question: Sputnik V vaccine is manufactured by using:
questions.explanation: Page 424: Sputnik V vaccine is manufactured by using vector base platform.

questions.question: Applications of biotechnology include:

options.B: Agriculture, therapeutic, diagnostic, biopharmaceutical, processed food, waste treatment, energy production

questions.explanation: Page 424: Applications include agriculture, therapeutic, diagnostic, biopharmaceutical, processed food, waste treatment and energy production.

questions.question: Calorie requirement for a female light worker is:
questions.explanation: Page 424: Female light worker requires 2100 calories.

questions.question: Calorie requirement for a male eight-hour worker is:
questions.explanation: Page 424: Male eight-hour worker requires 3000 calories.

questions.question: Calorie requirement for a female hard worker is:
questions.explanation: Page 424: Female hard worker requires 3000 calories.

questions.question: In the balanced diet chart, the grain (wheat, rice) requirement for an adult male doing normal work is:
questions.explanation: Page 424: For adult male moderate (M) worker, grain requirement is 520 g.

questions.question: Making curd, bread or wine are examples of:
questions.explanation: Page 424: Biotechnology examples include making curd, bread or wine in which microbes are used.

questions.question: Which technology creates unique character (antibiotic resistance) in bacteria?
questions.explanation: Page 424: Due to presence of plasmid, bacteria show resistance to many antibiotics, creating unique character.

questions.question: Restriction enzyme cuts DNA at:
questions.explanation: Page 424: Restriction enzyme cuts the DNA at specific location.

questions.question: The milk requirement for an adult male doing hard work is:
questions.explanation: Page 424: For adult male hard worker, milk requirement is 250 g.

questions.question: Pulses requirement for adult male normal worker is:
questions.explanation: Page 424: For adult male moderate worker, pulses requirement is 50 g.

questions.question: An example of GMO food crop approved in India is:
questions.explanation: Page 424: Bt-Brinjal is a genetically modified vegetable recently made available in Indian market.
